description:
You are the on-call deployment engineer responsible for rolling out the next version of the company’s data-pipeline code.

Current layout (already on disk):
• /home/user/data-pipeline/releases/v1.9/          – existing, “old” release  
• /home/user/data-pipeline/releases/v2.0/          – new release you must activate  
• /home/user/data-pipeline/current                 – symbolic link that still points at the v1.9 directory  
• /home/user/rollout.log                           – UTF-8 text file that already contains exactly one header line:

timestamp,from,to,status

What you have to do
1. Atomically redirect /home/user/data-pipeline/current so that, after your action, it resolves to /home/user/data-pipeline/releases/v2.0 (do not leave an intermediate broken link).
2. Append a single status line to /home/user/rollout.log in **one** operation, using only a **single shell command line** (you may chain commands with “&&”, “||”, “;”, subshells, etc., but everything must be typed as one logical line and executed once).  
   • The line must be appended (not inserted) after the existing header line.  
   • Format (comma-separated, **no spaces**):

     ISO8601_TIMESTAMP,OLD_TARGET,NEW_TARGET,success

     where  
       – ISO8601_TIMESTAMP is in UTC and must follow the exact pattern YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ (e.g. 2023-09-14T16:34:08Z).  
       – OLD_TARGET is the fully-qualified absolute path that /home/user/data-pipeline/current pointed to **before** you touched it.  
       – NEW_TARGET is /home/user/data-pipeline/releases/v2.0  
       – The fourth field is the literal word “success”.  
   • The line must end with a single trailing newline character “\n”.
3. Do not modify, remove, or rename any other files or lines.

Acceptance criteria checked by the automated grader
• readlink -f /home/user/data-pipeline/current returns /home/user/data-pipeline/releases/v2.0  
• /home/user/rollout.log still contains the original header line as its first line, and exactly one new line that matches the regular expression

  ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z,/home/user/data-pipeline/releases/v1\.9,/home/user/data-pipeline/releases/v2\.0,success\n$

• Only one new line has been added (file now has two lines in total).  
• Everything was performed via a single terminal command line.

Tip: standard POSIX tools (readlink, date -u, ln -sfn, etc.) are available. Editors or multi-line interactive tools are not required or desired.
truth:
Initial filesystem before agent starts:
1. Directories  
   /home/user/data-pipeline/  
   /home/user/data-pipeline/releases/  
   /home/user/data-pipeline/releases/v1.9/  
   /home/user/data-pipeline/releases/v2.0/

2. Marker files (content ends with newline):  
   /home/user/data-pipeline/releases/v1.9/VERSION  →  1.9\n  
   /home/user/data-pipeline/releases/v2.0/VERSION  →  2.0\n

3. Symlink  
   /home/user/data-pipeline/current  →  /home/user/data-pipeline/releases/v1.9

4. Log file (UTF-8, LF endings)  
   /home/user/rollout.log  →  timestamp,from,to,status\n

Expected final state:
1. Symlink /home/user/data-pipeline/current points to /home/user/data-pipeline/releases/v2.0  
   (checked with readlink -f).

2. /home/user/rollout.log now has exactly two lines.  
   Line 1 (unchanged): timestamp,from,to,status  
   Line 2 matches regex:  
   ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z,/home/user/data-pipeline/releases/v1\.9,/home/user/data-pipeline/releases/v2\.0,success\n$

No other files may be created, deleted, or modified.
